meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing & Version Control Strategies
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and solve version control issues. Advanced guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale development and revenue.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking pricing logic in static files. Integrate a robust version-controlled SaaS pricing architecture by leveraging modular configuration, automated schema validation via Antigravity, and Claude Code’s CLI for rapid, iterative deployment. This ensures your revenue logic remains audit-ready, scalable, and fully synchronized across all client production environments.
content.intro:
The cold sweat of a production outage hits differently when you realize your pricing logic was buried in a 'temporary' script with no git history. As a freelancer, your reputation is your currency; shipping code that lacks version control isn't just amateur—it's a liability that turns profitable SaaS builds into maintenance nightmares.

We are moving beyond the 'copy-paste' era. By treating your SaaS pricing engine as a version-controlled, immutable asset, you transform from a reactive coder into a proactive architect. Using the power of Antigravity for structural integrity and Claude Code for rapid implementation, we will build a pricing system that survives the chaos of rapid iteration.

steps.title: Define Schema-Driven Pricing
steps.description: Establish a JSON-based pricing schema to ensure all plans are strictly typed and versioned before logic is applied.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a strictly typed TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing engine. Include fields for tier, monthly/yearly rates, and feature flags. Ensure the structure allows for easy versioning in git.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing-schema.ts based on the provided Antigravity spec'

steps.title: Implement Versioned Logic
steps.description: Wrap your pricing calculations in versioned functions to allow for A/B testing or gradual rollouts of new pricing tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the pricing logic to support multiple versions. Create a factory pattern that selects the pricing strategy based on a version constant.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'git checkout -b feature/v2-pricing && npm run test:pricing'

steps.title: Automate Change Audits
steps.description: Configure your project to trigger a code audit whenever the pricing file is modified, ensuring no 'hotfix' goes unrecorded.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pre-commit hook script that validates pricing.json against the TypeScript schema to prevent invalid pricing deployments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add husky hook to validate pricing-schema.ts on commit'

steps.title: Deploy via Claude Code CLI
steps.description: Use Claude Code to push your changes and generate a semantic release note that highlights pricing modifications.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary of changes between the current pricing version and the previous one for a changelog.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'feat: update pricing tiers and add versioning constraints'

steps.title: Verify Deployment Integrity
steps.description: Run an automated test suite to ensure the new pricing logic integrates correctly with your existing payment provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that simulates a user upgrade path across all pricing tiers to ensure logic consistency.

pro_tips: Always store your pricing configuration in a separate repository to decouple revenue logic from UI code.
Use semantic versioning (SemVer) for your pricing tiers; never overwrite a tier, always deprecate and replace.
Implement a 'dry-run' endpoint that allows you to calculate pricing for a specific user segment without hitting the live DB.
Leverage Claude Code to maintain a 'Decision Log' markdown file, documenting why specific pricing changes were made.
Treat your pricing engine as an API-first service, even if it runs locally, to simplify future migration to microservices.
content.next_steps: After implementing your versioned pricing engine, set up a CI/CD pipeline to automate the deployment of these pricing updates, ensuring your production environment always reflects your latest repository state.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing your revenue logic and start architecting for scale. By controlling your pricing lifecycle, you save hours of debugging and build immense trust with your clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
